title: leonurus cardiaca subsp. villosus
intro: Leonurus cardiaca subsp. villosus, also known as the heart-leaved herb or wild sage, is a subspecies of Leonurus cardiaca, a perennial herb belonging to the mint family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and vasodilatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and lowering blood pressure due to its adaptogenic and cardiotonic properties. Leonurus cardiaca subsp. villosus can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and capsules, to harness its medicinal properties.
